tools.name: access_list_objects
tools.description: Lists database objects by type (table, module, form, report, query, macro, all). System tables (MSys*, ~*) are filtered out. Macros are fully supported as a regular object_type — combine with access_get_code / access_set_code / access_run_macro for end-to-end macro workflows.

tools.name: access_get_code
tools.description: Reads code/definition of an Access object. Modules: .bas code. Forms/reports: internal format (props + VBA). Macros: SaveAsText XML-like representation (Action/RunCommand blocks); pair with access_set_code to round-trip. Queries: SQL + DAO properties. For reading specific VBA procedures, prefer access_vbe_get_proc (faster, smaller output).

tools.name: access_set_code
tools.description: Imports code into the database. Overwrites if exists, creates if not. Call access_get_code first to read the original. For forms/reports: supports CodeBehindForm/CodeBehindReport (VBA is injected via VBE). For object_type='macro': accepts the SaveAsText representation (round-trip with access_get_code). For object_type='class_module': creates a VBA class module (adds VERSION 1.0 CLASS header automatically if missing). Automatic backup and restore on import failure.

tools.name: access_execute_sql
tools.description: Executes SQL via DAO. SELECT returns JSON rows (default limit: 500). INSERT/UPDATE return affected_rows. DELETE/DROP/ALTER require confirm_destructive=true.

tools.name: access_table_info
tools.description: Table structure via DAO: fields, types, size, required, record_count, is_linked.

tools.name: access_export_structure
tools.description: Generates Markdown with database structure: modules with signatures, forms, reports, queries, macros. Writes to disk and returns the content.

output_path.description: Output .md path (default: db_structure.md next to the DB)

tools.name: access_close
tools.description: Closes the COM session and releases the .accdb/.mdb file.

tools.name: access_vbe_get_lines
tools.description: Reads a range of lines from a VBA module via VBE COM. Provide either count or end_line (not both).

tools.name: access_vbe_get_proc
tools.description: Code of a VBA procedure by name. Returns start_line, body_line, count, code. start_line = VBE proc start, which INCLUDES the blank separator / comment lines above the proc (use it for whole-proc operations). body_line = the Sub/Function/Property declaration line itself (use it for line-range edits of the body).

tools.name: access_vbe_module_info
tools.description: Index of procedures in a VBA module: total_lines, procs [{name, start_line, body_line, count}]. start_line = VBE proc start (INCLUDES preceding blank/comment lines); body_line = the Sub/Function/Property declaration line.

tools.name: access_vbe_replace_lines
tools.description: Replaces lines in a VBA module via VBE. count=0: insertion. new_code='': deletion. Validates bounds automatically. Pass new code as new_code (a string) OR new_lines (a list of strings, joined with newlines — '' entries become blank lines). Batch mode: pass 'operations' (list of {start_line, count, new_code|new_lines}) to execute multiple operations in 1 call (auto-sorted bottom-to-top).

start_line.description: First line (1-based). Ignored if operations present.

count.description: Lines to delete (0 = insert). Ignored if operations present.

new_code.description: New code ('' = delete). Ignored if operations present.

new_lines.description: Alias for new_code as a list of lines (joined with '\n'; '' = blank line). Ignored if operations present.

operations.description: Batch mode: list of operations. Each: {start_line, count, new_code|new_lines}. Auto-sorted bottom-to-top.

tools.name: access_vbe_find
tools.description: Searches for text or regex in ONE specific VBA module. Returns matches [{line, content, proc}]. Each match includes 'proc' (procedure name). Optional: proc_name to limit search to a single procedure. To search ALL modules/forms/reports at once, use access_vbe_search_all instead.

search_text.description: Text or regex pattern to search

proc_name.description: Optional: limit search to this procedure

tools.name: access_vbe_search_all
tools.description: Searches for text or regex in ALL VBA modules (modules, forms, reports) in the database.

search_text.description: Text or regex pattern to search

tools.name: access_search_queries
tools.description: Searches for text or regex in the SQL of ALL queries. Returns [{query_name, sql}].

search_text.description: Text or regex pattern to search in SQL

tools.name: access_vbe_replace_proc
tools.description: Replaces an entire VBA procedure by name (boundaries found via VBE). Preserves the blank separator line above the proc. new_code='' DELETES the proc (and its leading blank separator) — this is the supported way to delete a procedure.

tools.name: access_vbe_patch_proc
tools.description: Surgical find/replace WITHIN a VBA procedure. More efficient than replace_proc when only a few lines change in a large proc. patches: [{find, replace}].

patches.description: List of find/replace to apply within the proc

tools.name: access_vbe_append
tools.description: Appends code to the end of a VBA module.

tools.name: access_list_controls
tools.description: Lists controls of a form/report with name, type, caption, control_source, position.

tools.name: access_get_control
tools.description: Full definition (Begin...End) of a control by name.

tools.name: access_create_control
tools.description: Creates a control on a form/report via COM. control_type: name or number (e.g.: 119=acCustomControl for ActiveX, 128=acWebBrowser native). Special props (case-insensitive): section (0=Detail,1=Header,2=Footer,3=PageHeader,4=PageFooter or name: 'detail','header','footer','reportheader','pageheader'...), parent (name of TabControl Page or OptionGroup), column_name, left, top, width, height. Pass parent='myTabPage' to drop the control inside a TabControl Page. Use control_name to rename the control after creation (or 'Name' in props). Other props that setattr() refuses are retried via the COM Properties collection before being recorded in property_errors. For ActiveX (type 119), use class_name with the ProgID (e.g.: 'Shell.Explorer.2').

control_name.description: Optional. Rename the auto-generated control (Command1/Text2/...) to this. Equivalent to passing 'Name' inside props (props takes precedence if both given).

props.description: Properties: section, parent, column_name, left, top, width, height (all case-insensitive), plus any COM property: Name, Caption, ControlSource, FontWeight, etc.

class_name.description: ProgID for ActiveX (type 119). E.g.: 'Shell.Explorer.2', 'MSCAL.Calendar.7'. Initializes the OLE control.

skip_lint.description: Suppress the embedded UI lint of the affected object (default false). Only for bulk programmatic edits.

snap_to_grid.description: Round left/top/width/height to the 60-twip design grid (default false). Values of -1 (auto) are left untouched.

tools.name: access_delete_control
tools.description: Deletes a control from a form/report via COM.

tools.name: access_export_text
tools.description: Exports form/report/module as text (SaveAsText). Does NOT open in Design view — does not recalculate positions. File is UTF-16 LE.

tools.name: access_import_text
tools.description: Imports form/report/module from text (LoadFromText). REPLACES if exists. Does NOT open in Design view — does not recalculate positions. For forms/reports with CodeBehindForm: separates VBA automatically and injects via VBE (same as access_set_code).

input_path.description: Input text file path (.txt) in UTF-16 LE format

tools.name: access_set_control_props
tools.description: Modifies properties of a control via COM. Numeric/boolean values are converted automatically.

props.description: Properties to modify: {Caption: 'X', Left: 1000, Visible: true, ...}

skip_lint.description: Suppress the embedded UI lint of the affected object (default false). Only for bulk programmatic edits.

snap_to_grid.description: Round any Left/Top/Width/Height in props to the 60-twip design grid (default false).

tools.name: access_set_form_property
tools.description: Sets form/report-level properties (RecordSource, Caption, DefaultView, HasModule, etc.) via COM in Design view.

props.description: Properties to modify: {RecordSource: 'Table', Caption: 'Title', HasModule: true, ...}

tools.name: access_get_db_property
tools.description: Reads a database property (CurrentDb.Properties) or Access option (GetOption).

tools.name: access_set_db_property
tools.description: Sets a database property or Access option. Creates the property if it does not exist.

prop_type.description: DAO type for CreateProperty (1=Boolean, 4=Long, 10=Text). Auto-detected if omitted

tools.name: access_list_linked_tables
tools.description: Lists linked tables with source_table, connect_string, is_odbc.

tools.name: access_relink_table
tools.description: Changes the connect string of a linked table and refreshes. relink_all=true updates all tables with the same original connection.

relink_all.description: true = relink all tables with the same original connection

tools.name: access_list_relationships
tools.description: Lists relationships between tables: name, tables, fields, cascade flags.

tools.name: access_create_relationship
tools.description: Creates a relationship between two tables. attributes: 256=cascade update, 4096=cascade delete (combinable with OR).

tools.name: access_list_references
tools.description: Lists VBA references: name, GUID, path, is_broken, built_in.

tools.name: access_manage_reference
tools.description: Adds (add) or removes (remove) a VBA reference. add: requires guid or path. remove: requires name.

tools.name: access_compact_repair
tools.description: Compacts and repairs the database. Closes, compacts to temp, replaces original and reopens. Returns sizes.

tools.name: access_decompile_compact
tools.description: Removes orphan VBA p-code via /decompile, recompiles and compacts. Typical reduction 60-70% on heavily edited front-end files. Use when the .accdb exceeds 30-40 MB without having local tables with data.

tools.name: access_manage_query
tools.description: Manages QueryDefs: create, modify, delete (requires confirm=true), rename, get_sql. create/modify require sql.

tools.name: access_list_indexes
tools.description: Lists indexes of a table: name, fields, primary, unique, foreign.

tools.name: access_manage_index
tools.description: Creates or deletes an index. create requires fields [{name, order?}]. primary/unique optional.

tools.name: access_compile_vba
tools.description: Compiles and saves all VBA modules. A dialog watchdog always runs and dismisses any error MsgBox; the watchdog is given `timeout` seconds (default 2) of grace after the compile call returns before being stopped.

timeout.description: Seconds to keep the dialog watchdog alive after triggering compile (default 2). Increase if compile errors surface late on slow machines.

tools.name: access_run_macro
tools.description: Executes an Access macro by name.

tools.name: access_output_report
tools.description: Exports a report to PDF, XLSX, RTF or TXT. output_path auto-generated if omitted. Refuses to overwrite an existing file unless overwrite=true.

overwrite.description: Replace existing file. Default false — prevents accidental clobber.

tools.name: access_transfer_data
tools.description: Import/export data between Access and Excel/CSV. file_type: xlsx or csv. range only for Excel, spec_name only for CSV.

tools.name: access_get_field_properties
tools.description: Reads all properties of a field: DefaultValue, ValidationRule, Description, Format, etc.

tools.name: access_set_field_property
tools.description: Sets a field property. Creates the property if it does not exist.

tools.name: access_list_startup_options
tools.description: Lists the 14 common startup options with their current values.

tools.name: access_create_database
tools.description: Creates an empty Access database (.accdb). Error if the file already exists.

tools.name: access_create_table
tools.description: Creates an Access table via DAO with full support: types, defaults, descriptions and primary key — all in a single call. More robust than CREATE TABLE via SQL, which does not support DEFAULT or YESNO in Jet DDL. Each field accepts: name, type, size, required, primary_key, default, description. Valid types: autonumber, long, integer, short, byte, text, memo, currency, double, single, datetime, boolean/yesno/bit, guid, ole, bigint.

fields.description: List of fields [{name, type, size?, required?, primary_key?, default?, description?}]

tools.name: access_alter_table
tools.description: Modifies the structure of an Access table via DAO. Actions: add_field (with type, size, default, description), delete_field (requires confirm=true), rename_field. More robust than ALTER TABLE via SQL in Jet.

tools.name: access_create_form
tools.description: Creates a new form in the database. Avoids the 'Save As' MsgBox that blocks COM when using CreateForm() directly. Optional has_header (header/footer section), record_source (bind to table/query) and default_view (0=Single, 1=Continuous, 2=Datasheet, 3=PivotTable, 4=PivotChart, 5=Split, 6=Split datasheet).

form_name.description: Name of the form to create

record_source.description: Optional: table/query name to bind the form to (sets RecordSource)

tools.name: access_build_form
tools.description: Builds a complete, well-laid-out form from a DECLARATIVE spec — the preferred way to create data-entry forms. You describe WHAT goes on the form (a title, an ordered list of fields, a row of action buttons, single or two-column); the tool computes every Left/Top/Width/Height from a canonical 60-twip grid, applies a WCAG-safe palette (or a curated design direction via theme=despacho|panel|archivo), assigns a sane tab order and sizes the form and its header/footer sections. You never pick a coordinate. Use this instead of many access_create_control calls; fine-tune afterwards with access_set_control_props if needed. See access_tips('layout') for the numbers and access_tips('design') for the directions.

form_name.description: Name of the form to create

record_source.description: Optional table/query to bind the form to. Fields with a matching name get a ControlSource.

title.description: Optional. Adds a form-header band with this caption (bold dark title, readable on the themed header) and sets the form Caption.

fields.description: Ordered fields. Each item is a string (bound textbox) OR an object: {field, label, control(textbox|memo|combobox|listbox|checkbox|date), name, control_source, row_source, width_units (single-column only), height, props}.

actions.description: Footer buttons. Each item is a string (caption) OR an object {caption, name, on_click, props}.

layout.description: single = one label+field per row; two-column = two pairs per row (width_units ignored).

theme.description: Visual style. Basics: light = palette+fonts on the default look; plain = geometry only; polish = Segoe UI + more air + chrome off; flat = accent band + card + grey canvas. Curated DESIGN DIRECTIONS (coherent typeface + type scale + WCAG-verified palette + density, all with an accent header band): despacho (serif Constantia title on warm paper), panel (Segoe UI Semibold, white card on a cool canvas), archivo (serif Cambria title, warm editorial, spacious). See access_tips('design') for the full guide.

overwrite.description: Delete an existing form of the same name first.

skip_lint.description: Suppress the embedded UI lint of the result (default false).

tools.name: access_delete_object
tools.description: Deletes an Access object (module, form, report, query, macro). Requires confirm=true.

object_name.description: Name of the object to delete

tools.name: access_run_vba
tools.description: Executes a VBA Sub/Function. Supports 3 syntaxes: 'Module.MySub' (standard module via Application.Run), 'MySub' (same), 'Forms.FormName.Method' (form module via COM — form must be open). Returns result if Function. With timeout, automatically dismisses MsgBox/InputBox if exceeded.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. If exceeded, automatically dismisses MsgBox/InputBox dialogs and returns error

tools.name: access_eval_vba
tools.description: Evaluates a VBA/Access expression via Application.Eval. Works with: domain functions (DLookup, DCount...), built-in VBA functions (Date(), Len()...), form properties of OPEN forms (Forms!frmX.Prop), public functions in standard modules. Does NOT work with: class instances (even default/predeclared), variables, Subs. For class instances use access_run_vba with a wrapper function, or this tool will attempt an automatic fallback via a temp VBA module.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. If the expression (or its temp-module fallback) blocks on a MsgBox/InputBox or other modal dialog, it is auto-dismissed and an error is returned -- same behaviour as access_run_vba

tools.name: access_delete_relationship
tools.description: Deletes a relationship between tables by name. Requires confirm=true — this is irreversible.

name.description: Name of the relationship to delete

confirm.description: Must be true to actually delete — safety guard.

tools.name: access_find_usages
tools.description: Searches for text or regex in VBA, query SQL and control properties (ControlSource, RecordSource, RowSource, SourceObject, DefaultValue, ValidationRule, LinkChildFields, LinkMasterFields). Returns results grouped: vba_matches, query_matches, control_matches.

search_text.description: Text or regex pattern to search

tools.name: access_find_definition
tools.description: Go-to-definition for a VBA symbol — the mirror of access_find_usages. Scans every standard module, form code-behind and report code-behind for DECLARATIONS of `symbol` and returns where each lives (object_type, object_name, line, declaration, scope, value if constant). Detects: const, enum, enum_member, type, type_field, sub, function, property (Get/Let/Set, incl. Default Property), declare (Win32 API), and module-level variable. Handles multi-const lines (Const A=1, B=2) and joins VBA line continuations (` _` at end of line). Use this when you need the value of a constant/enum member, or the source of a procedure, before deciding what to edit next. Case-insensitive by default.

kinds.description: Optional whitelist. Default: all kinds. E.g. ['const','enum_member'] to resolve only constant-like names.

match_case.description: VBA is case-insensitive, so leave False unless you know otherwise.

scan_types.description: Which object types to scan. Default: all three. Pass ['module'] when you know the symbol is a public declaration in a standard module — it's much faster on large DBs because scanning form/report code-behind triggers a Design-view open/close when the VBE cache is cold.

first_only.description: Stop after the first match. Useful for unique names in big databases.

tools.name: access_execute_batch
tools.description: Executes multiple SQL statements in a single call. Each statement can be SELECT (returns rows, default limit 100 — adjustable via 'limit') or INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (returns affected_rows). stop_on_error=true stops at first error. DELETE/DROP/TRUNCATE/ALTER require confirm_destructive=true.

statements.description: List of SQL statements [{sql: str, label?: str}, ...]

label.description: Optional label to identify the statement

stop_on_error.description: true = stop at first error (default: true)

limit.description: Max rows returned per SELECT statement (1-10000, default 100)

tools.name: access_get_form_property
tools.description: Reads properties of a form or report (RecordSource, Caption, DefaultView, HasModule, etc.). object_type ('form' or 'report') is required. If property_names is omitted, returns all readable properties.

property_names.description: List of properties to read. Omit to read all.

tools.name: access_set_multiple_controls
tools.description: Modifies properties of multiple controls on a form/report in a single operation. Opens in Design view once, applies changes, saves and closes.

controls.description: List of controls [{name: str, props: {prop: val}}, ...]

skip_lint.description: Suppress the embedded UI lint of the affected object (default false). Only for bulk programmatic edits.

tools.name: access_lint_form
tools.description: NOT a designer and NOT aesthetics — expect zero 'good design' from this. It is a deterministic checker of the OBVIOUS, mechanically-verifiable stuff only (a seatbelt, not a stylist). Returns structured JSON violations the agent should fix BEFORE declaring a layout done: contrast (WCAG white-on-white etc.), overlap, out-of-bounds, text truncation, sibling inconsistency, misalignment, zero-size/invisible. summary.verdict is PASS / REVIEW / FAIL. Static (one export, no Design view). measure='auto' tries Access WizHook for exact text width and falls back to a heuristic. The same engine runs automatically on every control mutation (see the 'lint' block in those tools' results).

rules.description: Subset of rule ids to run; omit for all. Ids: contrast, overlap, out_of_bounds, truncation, sibling_inconsistency, misalignment, invisible_or_zero_size, grid_alignment, spacing_consistency, edge_margin, hierarchy, generic_font (the last 5 are info-only design-quality checks).

measure.description: Text-width source for truncation. 'wizhook' = exact (needs compiled VBA); 'heuristic' = no COM; 'auto' tries wizhook then falls back. Default 'auto'.

include_screenshot.description: Also capture a PNG of the form and return its path (default false).

max_violations.description: Cap on returned violations (default 200; info dropped first, errors kept).

tools.name: access_tips
tools.description: Tips and gotchas for working with Access via MCP. Topics: eval, controls, gotchas, sql, vbe, compile, design, lint, macros, subform_tabcontrol. Without topic returns the list.

topic.description: Topic: eval, controls, gotchas, sql, vbe, compile, design (empty = list of topics)

tools.name: access_screenshot
tools.description: Captures the Access window as PNG. Optionally opens a form/report before capturing. Returns path, dimensions and metadata. wait_ms pumps Windows messages (Timer events, ActiveX init).

object_type.description: Object type to open before capturing (optional)

wait_ms.description: Wait in ms after opening object (0 = instant)

open_timeout_sec.description: Max seconds waiting for OpenForm/OpenReport. If Form_Load takes longer (slow OpenRecordset), ESC is sent and error is raised. Default 30.

tools.name: access_ui_click
tools.description: Click at image coordinates on the Access window. Coordinates are relative to a previous screenshot (image_width required for scaling).

image_width.description: Screenshot width used for the coordinates

tools.name: access_search_data
tools.description: Searches a text string in any Text/Memo field of any LOCAL table. Skips system tables (MSys*, ~*) and linked tables. Returns matches grouped by table with an excerpt around each hit. Use `tables` to restrict the scan to a list of table names. Jet LIKE is case-insensitive by default; pass match_case=true to filter the result set further. Results are capped per-table and total.

search_text.description: Text to find (substring match, case-insensitive by default)

tables.description: Optional. Restrict to these tables. Default: all local non-system tables.

max_results_total.description: Cap matches across the whole search (default: 500)

match_case.description: If true, filters out rows where Jet matched only by case-folding

tools.name: access_clone_object
tools.description: Clones a form, report, module, class_module, query or macro to a new name. Internally uses SaveAsText → LoadFromText, preserving binary sections (PrtMip, PrtDevMode, NameMap, GUID...) and VBA code-behind. Refuses to overwrite an existing target unless overwrite=true.

overwrite.description: Delete target first if it already exists

tools.name: access_manage_tab_order
tools.description: Reads or sets the TabIndex of controls on a form/report. Actions: 'get' (returns controls grouped by section, ordered by TabIndex), 'set' (assigns TabIndex 0..N-1 in the order of `tab_order`), 'auto_renumber' (re-sequences current TabIndex values to be contiguous 0..N-1 — useful after deleting controls). Skips controls that don't support TabIndex (Label, Line, Rectangle, Image, PageBreak).

tab_order.description: [set] Ordered list of control names — TabIndex assigned 0..N-1 in this order

section.description: Optional. Restrict the operation to a single section name (e.g. 'Detail', 'FormHeader')

tools.name: access_ui_type
tools.description: Types text or sends keyboard shortcuts to the Access window. Use 'text' for normal text, 'key' for special keys (enter, tab, escape, f1-f12, etc.), 'modifiers' for combinations (ctrl, shift, alt).

key.description: Special key: enter, tab, escape, backspace, delete, up, down, left, right, home, end, f1-f12, space, pageup, pagedown

modifiers.description: Modifiers: ctrl, shift, alt, ctrl+shift — combined with key

prompts.name: access-workflow
prompts.description: Usage instructions for the MCP access server for working with Microsoft Access databases (.accdb/.mdb) from Claude Code.

arguments.name: db_path
arguments.description: Full path to the .accdb or .mdb file

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'access_export_structure' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'access_export_structure' accepts a file path parameter (output_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): output_path | description: Generates Markdown with database structure: modules with signatures, forms, reports, queries, macros. Writes to disk and returns the content.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'access_export_text' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'access_export_text' accepts a file path parameter (output_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): output_path | description: Exports form/report/module as text (SaveAsText). Does NOT open in Design view — does not recalculate positions. File is UTF-16 LE.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'access_output_report' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'access_output_report' accepts a file path parameter (output_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): output_path | description: Exports a report to PDF, XLSX, RTF or TXT. output_path auto-generated if omitted. Refuses to overwrite an existing file unless overwrite=true.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'access_transfer_data' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'access_transfer_data' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence: path param(s): file_path | description: Import/export data between Access and Excel/CSV. file_type: xlsx or csv. range only for Excel, spec_name only for CSV.
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Parameter 'value' in 'access_set_db_property' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'value'.

findings.title: Parameter 'value' in 'access_set_field_property' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'value'.

findings.title: Parameter 'default' in 'access_alter_table' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'default'.

findings.title: 'access_tips' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'access_execute_sql' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'access_execute_sql' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Executes SQL via DAO. SELECT returns JSON rows (default limit: 500). INSERT/UPDATE return affected_rows. DELETE/DROP/ALTER require confirm_destructive=true.
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'access_vbe_replace_lines' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'access_vbe_replace_lines' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Replaces lines in a VBA module via VBE. count=0: insertion. new_code='': deletion. Validates bounds automatically. Pass new code as new_code (a string) OR new_lines (a list of strings, joined with newlines — '' entries become blank lines). Batch mode: pass 'operations' (list of {start_line, count, n
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'access_run_macro' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'access_run_macro' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Executes an Access macro by name.
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'access_run_vba' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'access_run_vba' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Executes a VBA Sub/Function. Supports 3 syntaxes: 'Module.MySub' (standard module via Application.Run), 'MySub' (same), 'Forms.FormName.Method' (form module via COM — form must be open). Returns result if Function. With timeout, automatically dismisses MsgBox/InputBox if exceeded.
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'access_execute_batch' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'access_execute_batch' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Executes multiple SQL statements in a single call. Each statement can be SELECT (returns rows, default limit 100 — adjustable via 'limit') or INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (returns affected_rows). stop_on_error=true stops at first error. DELETE/DROP/TRUNCATE/ALTER require confirm_destructive=true.
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 66 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Prompt template injection confirmed in 'access-workflow'
findings.description: prompts/get for 'access-workflow' reflected the injection payload verbatim in the returned messages, confirming unsanitized argument reflection.
findings.evidence:
Payload reflected in messages: {"description": "Required workflow for working with Access databases", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {"type": "text", "text": "
I'm working with a Microsoft Access database: CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}

REQUIRED RULES for the agent:
1. Any operation on .accdb or .mdb files MUST be d
findings.remediation: Sanitize prompt arguments before embedding them in message templates.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":44,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Input validation error: 'test'
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: Server accepts requests without jsonrpc field
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request missing the required 'jsonrpc' field. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, this field is mandatory and must equal '2.0'.
findings.remediation: Validate that the 'jsonrpc' field is present and equals '2.0' before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for non-conformant messages.

findings.title: Server accepts array request ID — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request with an array as the request ID. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires the id to be a string, number, or null.
findings.remediation: Validate that the request 'id' is a string, number, or null. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for array or object IDs.
